segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Dancing is also a sociable activity, which is another reason it makes us feel good.
segments.zh: 跳舞也是一种社交活动，这也是它能让我们感觉良好的另一个原因。一项研究比较了人们在家对着视频跳舞和在工作室里集体跳舞的愉悦感。集体跳舞的人报告说感觉更快乐，而独自跳舞的人则没有。在另一项实验中，约克和谢菲尔德大学的研究人员召集了一组学生，让他们每人进入一个播放五分钟音乐的实验室。每个人必须从三个选项中选择：安静地坐着听音乐，边听边骑健身车，或者站起来跳舞。所有人在实验前后都完成了认知任务。结果表明，那些选择跳舞的人在解决问题时表现出更高的创造力。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One study compared people's enjoyment of dancing at home in front of a video with dancing in a group in a studio.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The people dancing in a group reported feeling happier, whereas those dancing alone did not.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In another experiment, university researchers at York and Sheffield took a group of students and sent each of them into a lab where music was played for five minutes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Each had to choose from three options: to sit and listen quietly to the music, to cycle on an exercise bike while they listened, or to get up and dance.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: All were given cognitive tasks to perform before and after.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The result showed that those who chose to dance showed much more creativity when doing problem-solving tasks.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Doctor Lovatt at the University of Hertfordshire believes dance could be a very useful way to help people suffering from mental health problems.
segments.zh: 赫特福德大学的洛瓦特博士认为，跳舞可能是帮助有心理健康问题的人的一种非常有效的方式。他认为跳舞应该被作为疗法来处方，帮助人们克服抑郁等问题。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: He thinks dance should be prescribed as therapy to help people overcome issues such as depression.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's well established that dance is a good way of encouraging adolescent girls to take exercise but what about older people?
segments.zh: 众所周知，跳舞是鼓励青春期女孩锻炼的好方法，但老年人呢？研究表明，对于六十岁及以上的人来说，跳舞有巨大的好处。跳舞的一大优点是参与没有障碍。任何人都可以尝试，即使是那些体能水平较低的人。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Studies have shown that there are enormous benefits for people in their sixties and beyond.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One of the great things about dance is that there are no barriers to participation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Anyone can have a go, even those whose standard of fitness is quite low.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Dance can be especially beneficial for older adults who can't run or do more intense workouts, or for those who don't want to.
segments.zh: 跳舞对那些不能跑步或进行高强度锻炼的老年人，或者不想这样做的人尤其有益。2015年的一项研究发现，即使是温和的舞蹈锻炼也有助于促进心脏健康。而且有大量证据表明，跳舞通过帮助人们改善平衡能力，降低了跌倒的风险，而跌倒可能导致例如髋部骨折。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One 2015 study found that even a gentle dance workout helps to promote a healthy heart.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And there's plenty of evidence which suggests that dancing lowers the risk of falls, which could result in a broken hip, for example, by helping people to improve their balance.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are some less obvious benefits of dance for older people too.
segments.zh: 跳舞对老年人还有一些不那么明显的好处。在研究这个主题之前，我没有意识到的一件事是，跳舞不仅仅是身体上的挑战。它还需要大量的注意力，因为你需要记住不同的舞步和套路。对于老年人来说，这种活动尤其重要，因为它迫使大脑更快地处理信息并记住更多信息。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One thing I hadn't realised before researching this topic was that dance isn't just a physical challenge.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It also requires a lot of concentration because you need to remember different steps and routines.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For older people, this kind of activity is especially important because it forces their brain to process things more quickly and to retain more information.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Current research also shows that dance promotes a general sense of well-being in older participants, which can last up to a week after a class.
segments.zh: 当前的研究还表明，跳舞能促进老年参与者的整体幸福感，这种幸福感在课后可以持续长达一周。参与者报告说感觉不那么疲劳，并且更有动力去更活跃地做日常活动，比如园艺或步行去商店或公园。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Participants report feeling less tired and having greater motivation to be more active and do daily activities such as gardening or walking to the shops or a park.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ballroom or country dancing, both popular with older people, have to be done in groups.
segments.zh: 交谊舞或乡村舞，两者都受老年人欢迎，必须集体进行。它们需要合作，并且通常涉及与舞伴接触，所有这些都鼓励在舞池中的互动。这有助于发展新的关系，并可以减少老年人的孤独感，这在许多国家是一个大问题。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They require collaboration and often involve touching a dance partner, all of which encourages interaction on the dance floor.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This helps to develop new relationships and can reduce older people's sense of isolation, which is a huge problem in many countries.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I also looked at the benefits of Zumba.
segments.zh: 我还研究了尊巴舞的好处。现在有180个国家的1500万人定期参加尊巴舞课程，这是一种基于拉丁舞动作的有氧运动。威斯康星大学运动与体育科学教授约翰·波卡里分析了一组经常跳尊巴舞的女性，发现一节40分钟的课程能燃烧大约370卡路里。这与中等强度的运动如阶梯有氧或跆拳道相似。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Fifteen million people in 180 countries now regularly take a Zumba class, an aerobic workout based on Latin American dance moves.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: John Porcari, a professor of exercise and sport science at the University of Wisconsin, analysed a group of women who were Zumba regulars and found that a class lasting 40 minutes burns about 370 calories.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is similar to moderately intense exercises like step aerobics or kickboxing.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A study in the American Journal of Health Behavior showed that when women with obesity did Zumba three times a week for 16 weeks, they lost an average of 1.2 kilos and lowered their percentage of body fat by 1%.
segments.zh: 《美国健康行为杂志》上的一项研究表明，肥胖女性每周跳三次尊巴，坚持16周，平均减重1.2公斤，体脂率降低1%。更重要的是，她们非常喜欢这门课，以至于养成了习惯，继续每周至少上一次课——这对有氧运动项目来说非常罕见。在体能提升方面，舞蹈永远无法与高强度训练相提并论，但它很可能越来越受欢迎，因为这是一种有趣的健身方式。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: More importantly, the women enjoyed the class so much that they made it a habit and continued to attend classes at least once a week – very unusual for an aerobic exercise programme.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Dance is never going to compete with high-intensity workouts when it comes to physical fitness gains, but its popularity is likely to keep on rising because it's such a fun way to keep fit.

items.prompt: An experiment on university students suggested that dance increases 31

pairs.note: 原文用'showed much more creativity'对应题干'increases creativity'，都是说跳舞提升创造力。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'The result showed that those who chose to dance showed much more creativity'，其中'creativity'直接对应题干中的'creativity'，而'showed much more'替换了'increases'。

items.prompt: For those with mental illness, dance could be used as a form of 32

pairs.note: mental illness 和 mental health problems 是同义替换，都指心理健康问题。

pairs.q: dance could be used as a form of
pairs.note: 题干说'被用作一种形式'，原文说'被开处方作为'，都是指舞蹈被用于治疗。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到舞蹈应被开处方作为疗法来帮助有心理健康问题的人，其中'therapy'直接对应答案，而'mental health problems'替换了题干中的'mental illness'。

items.prompt: accessible for people with low levels of 33

pairs.note: 题干说'accessible'（可接近的），原文说'no barriers'（没有障碍），意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'no barriers to participation'和'standard of fitness is quite low'，与题干'accessible for people with low levels of fitness'对应，答案就是fitness。

items.prompt: better 34 reduces the risk of accidents

pairs.note: 原文用'falls'（跌倒）具体化了题干中的'accidents'（事故），属于同义替换。
题干中的'better 34'指代'balance'，原文直接出现'improve their balance'，是答案的定位词。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'better 34 reduces the risk of accidents'，原文提到'dancing lowers the risk of falls...by helping people to improve their balance'，其中'balance'就是答案，'lowers the risk of falls'对应'reduces the risk of accidents'。

items.prompt: improves 35 function by making it work faster

pairs.p: forces their brain to process things more quickly and to retain more information
pairs.note: 题干说'改善功能，使其工作更快'，原文说'迫使大脑更快处理并记住更多信息'，是具体解释
答案词'brain'在原文中直接出现，是定位关键

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'改善35功能'，原文提到'forces their brain to process things more quickly'，其中'brain'就是答案，且'process more quickly'对应'work faster'。

items.prompt: gives people more 36 to take exercise

pairs.p: greater motivation to be more active
pairs.note: 'more' 对应 'greater'，'take exercise' 对应 'be more active'，都是表达增加运动的意思。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'greater motivation to be more active'，其中 'greater motivation' 对应题干中的 'gives people more ... to take exercise'，因此答案就是 motivation。

items.prompt: can lessen the feeling of 37, very common in older people

pairs.note: lessen 和 reduce 同义，feeling 和 sense 同义

pairs.p: which is a huge problem in many countries
pairs.note: 题干说老年人中很常见，原文说在很多国家是个大问题，意思相近

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'reduce older people's sense of isolation'，其中 'reduce' 对应题干 'lessen'，'sense of' 对应 'feeling of'，因此答案就是 isolation。

items.prompt: A study at The University of Wisconsin showed that doing Zumba for 40 minutes uses up as many 38 as other quite intense forms of exercise.

pairs.note: uses up 和 burns 都表示消耗，是同义替换。
题干中的数字38是题号，实际对应原文中的370 calories。

pairs.q: other quite intense forms of exercise
pairs.p: moderately intense exercises like step aerobics or kickboxing
pairs.note: 原文用 moderately intense exercises 举例，与题干中的 quite intense forms 对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 Zumba 课燃烧约370卡路里，与中等强度运动相似，因此答案应为 calories。

items.prompt: women suffering from 39 benefited from doing Zumba.

pairs.note: 'suffering from' 和 'with' 都表示患有某种疾病，是同义替换。
题干中的数字39对应答案obesity，但原文直接出现obesity，所以是原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位时先找题干中的关键词'women'和'Zumba'，原文第一句就出现了'women with obesity'和'did Zumba'，直接对应。

items.prompt: Zumba became a 40 for the participants.

pairs.note: 题干中'became a 40'指代一个名词，原文'made it a habit'表示养成了习惯，'habit'就是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到女性喜欢Zumba并养成了习惯，'made it a habit'直接对应答案'habit'。
